[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

### Residential Proxies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#residential-proxies "Link to this heading")

[![Thordata](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/thordata.png)](https://www.thordata.com/?ls=github&lk=curl_cffi)

[Thordata](https://www.thordata.com/?ls=github&lk=curl_cffi)
: A reliable and cost-effective proxy service provider. One-click collection of public network data, providing enterprises and developers with stable, efficient, and compliant global proxy IP services. Register for a free trial of [residential proxies](https://www.thordata.com/products/residential-proxies/?ls=github&lk=curl_cffi)
 and receive 2000 free SERP API calls.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#features "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#id3 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| native retry | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Full http/3 supported was added in v0.12.0.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#install "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quick_start.html)
.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#documentation "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quick_start.html)
. Then the impersonate.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html)
.

If you have any questions, be sure to check out the [FAQ](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/faq.html)
 section before opening an issue.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#features "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#id2 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Full http/3 supported was added in v0.12.0.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#install "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/quick_start.html)
.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/#documentation "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/quick_start.html)
. Then the impersonate.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html)
.

If you have any questions, be sure to check out the [FAQ](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/faq.html)
 section before opening an issue.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#id2 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Full http/3 supported was added in v0.12.0.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/quick_start.html)
.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/#documentation "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/quick_start.html)
. Then the impersonate.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/api.html)
.

If you have any questions, be sure to check out the [FAQ](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/faq.html)
 section before opening an issue.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

* * *

[![Scrapfly](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/scrapfly.png)](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)

[Scrapfly](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)
 is an enterprise-grade solution providing Web Scraping API that aims to simplify the scraping process by managing everything: real browser rendering, rotating proxies, and fingerprints (TLS, HTTP, browser) to bypass all major anti-bots. Scrapfly also unlocks the observability by providing an analytical dashboard and measuring the success rate/block rate in details.

Scrapfly is a good solution if you are looking for a cloud-managed solution for `curl_cffi`. If you are managing TLS/HTTP fingerprint by yourself with `curl_cffi`, they also maintain a [curl to python converter](https://scrapfly.io/web-scraping-tools/curl-python/curl_cffi)
 .

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#features "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#id3 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#install "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import requests

url \= "https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= requests.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

Sponsor[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#sponsor "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### ScrapeNinja[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/#scrapeninja "Link to this heading")

[![ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/img/logo_with_text_new5.svg)](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)

[ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)
 is a web scraping API with two engines: fast, with high performance and TLS fingerprint; and slower with a real browser under the hood.

ScrapeNinja handles headless browsers, proxies, timeouts, retries, and helps with data extraction, so you can just get the data in JSON. Rotating proxies are available out of the box on all subscription plans.

[中文 README](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/blob/main/README-zh.md)
 | [Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

* * *

[![Scrapfly](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/main/assets/scrapfly.png)](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)

[Scrapfly](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)
 is an enterprise-grade solution providing Web Scraping API that aims to simplify the scraping process by managing everything: real browser rendering, rotating proxies, and fingerprints (TLS, HTTP, browser) to bypass all major anti-bots. Scrapfly also unlocks the observability by providing an analytical dashboard and measuring the success rate/block rate in details.

Scrapfly is a good solution if you are looking for a cloud-managed solution for `curl_cffi`. If you are managing TLS/HTTP fingerprint by yourself with `curl_cffi`, they also maintain a [curl to python converter](https://scrapfly.io/web-scraping-tools/curl-python/curl_cffi)
 .

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#features "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#id3 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#install "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import requests

url \= "https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= requests.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### ScrapeNinja[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.1/#scrapeninja "Link to this heading")

[![ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/img/logo_with_text_new5.svg)](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)

[ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)
 is a web scraping API with two engines: fast, with high performance and TLS fingerprint; and slower with a real browser under the hood.

ScrapeNinja handles headless browsers, proxies, timeouts, retries, and helps with data extraction, so you can just get the data in JSON. Rotating proxies are available out of the box on all subscription plans.

[中文 README](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/blob/main/README-zh.md)
 | [Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

* * *

[![Scrapfly](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/main/assets/scrapfly.png)](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)

[Scrapfly](https://scrapfly.io/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=sponsoring&utm_campaign=curl_cffi)
 is an enterprise-grade solution providing Web Scraping API that aims to simplify the scraping process by managing everything: real browser rendering, rotating proxies, and fingerprints (TLS, HTTP, browser) to bypass all major anti-bots. Scrapfly also unlocks the observability by providing an analytical dashboard and measuring the success rate/block rate in details.

Scrapfly is a good solution if you are looking for a cloud-managed solution for `curl_cffi`. If you are managing TLS/HTTP fingerprint by yourself with `curl_cffi`, they also maintain a [curl to python converter](https://scrapfly.io/web-scraping-tools/curl-python/curl_cffi)
 .

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#features "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#id3 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#install "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import requests

url \= "https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= requests.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= requests.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

Sponsor[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#sponsor "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### ScrapeNinja[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/#scrapeninja "Link to this heading")

[![ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/img/logo_with_text_new5.svg)](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)

[ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)
 is a web scraping API with two engines: fast, with high performance and TLS fingerprint; and slower with a real browser under the hood.

ScrapeNinja handles headless browsers, proxies, timeouts, retries, and helps with data extraction, so you can just get the data in JSON. Rotating proxies are available out of the box on all subscription plans.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [\`curl-impersonate fork\`\_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#id5)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### SerpAPI[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#serpapi "Link to this heading")

[![SerpAPI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/serpapi.png)](https://serpapi.com/)

Scrape Google and other search engines from [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/)
’s fast, easy, and complete API. 0.66s average response time (≤ 0.5s for Ludicrous Speed Max accounts), 99.95% SLAs, pay for successful responses only.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### Easy Captcha Bypass for Scraping[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#easy-captcha-bypass-for-scraping "Link to this heading")

[![Capsolver](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/capsolver.jpg)](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

[CapSolver](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)
 is an AI-powered tool that easily bypasses Captchas, allowing uninterrupted access to public data. It supports a variety of Captchas and works seamlessly with `curl_cffi`, Puppeteer, Playwright, and more. Fast, reliable, and cost-effective. Plus, `curl_cffi` users can use the code **“CURL”** to get an extra 6% balance! and register [here](https://dashboard.capsolver.com/passport/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#id4 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ☑️  | ☑️  |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl to make it work, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Since v0.11.0. However, only Linux and macOS are supported, Windows is not supported due to failed building of ngtcp2.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

import curl\_cffi

url \= "https://tls.browserleaks.com/json"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= curl\_cffi.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

async with curl\_cffi.AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

\# asyncio
import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    ws \= await s.ws\_connect("wss://echo.websocket.org")
    await asyncio.gather(\*\[ws.send\_str("Hello, World!") for \_ in range(10)\])
    async for message in ws:
        print(message)

# Welcome to curl_cffi’s documentation! — curl_cffi  documentation

Welcome to curl\_cffi’s documentation![¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#welcome-to-curl-cffi-s-documentation "Link to this heading")

====================================================================================================================================================

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [\`curl-impersonate fork\`\_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#id5)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### SerpAPI[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#serpapi "Link to this heading")

[![SerpAPI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/serpapi.png)](https://serpapi.com/)

Scrape Google and other search engines from [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/)
’s fast, easy, and complete API. 0.66s average response time (≤ 0.5s for Ludicrous Speed Max accounts), 99.95% SLAs, pay for successful responses only.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### Easy Captcha Bypass for Scraping[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#easy-captcha-bypass-for-scraping "Link to this heading")

[![Capsolver](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/capsolver.jpg)](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

[CapSolver](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)
 is an AI-powered tool that easily bypasses Captchas, allowing uninterrupted access to public data. It supports a variety of Captchas and works seamlessly with `curl_cffi`, Puppeteer, Playwright, and more. Fast, reliable, and cost-effective. Plus, `curl_cffi` users can use the code **“CURL”** to get an extra 6% balance! and register [here](https://dashboard.capsolver.com/passport/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#id4 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ☑️  | ☑️  |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl to make it work, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Since v0.11.0. However, only Linux and macOS are supported, Windows is not supported due to failed building of ngtcp2.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

import curl\_cffi

url \= "https://tls.browserleaks.com/json"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= curl\_cffi.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

async with curl\_cffi.AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.2/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

\# asyncio
import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    ws \= await s.ws\_connect("wss://echo.websocket.org")
    await asyncio.gather(\*\[ws.send\_str("Hello, World!") for \_ in range(10)\])
    async for message in ws:
        print(message)

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [\`curl-impersonate fork\`\_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#id4)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

* * *

[![ProxyCurl](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/proxycurl.png)](https://nubela.co/proxycurl/?utm_campaign=influencer_marketing&utm_source=github&utm_medium=social&utm_term=-&utm_content=lexiforest-curl_cffi)

Scrape public LinkedIn profile data at scale with [Proxycurl APIs](https://nubela.co/proxycurl/?utm_campaign=influencer_marketing&utm_source=github&utm_medium=social&utm_term=-&utm_content=lexiforest-curl_cffi)
. Built for developers, by developers.

*   GDPR, CCPA, SOC2 compliant
    
*   High rate limit (300 requests/min), Fast (APIs respond in ~2s), High accuracy
    
*   Fresh data - 88% of data is scraped real-time, other 12% is <29 days
    
*   Tons of data points returned per profile
    

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#id3 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

import curl\_cffi

url \= "https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tools.scrapfly.io/api/fp/ja3", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= requests.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### ScrapeNinja[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.10.0/#scrapeninja "Link to this heading")

[![ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/img/logo_with_text_new5.svg)](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)

[ScrapeNinja](https://scrapeninja.net/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=cffi)
 is a web scraping API with two engines: fast, with high performance and TLS fingerprint; and slower with a real browser under the hood.

ScrapeNinja handles headless browsers, proxies, timeouts, retries, and helps with data extraction, so you can just get the data in JSON. Rotating proxies are available out of the box on all subscription plans.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [\`curl-impersonate fork\`\_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#id5)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

’s fast, easy, and complete API. 0.66s average response time (≤ 0.5s for Ludicrous Speed Max accounts), 99.95% SLAs, pay for successful responses only.

* * *

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#id4 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ☑️  | ☑️  |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl to make it work, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Since v0.11.0. However, only Linux and macOS are supported, Windows is not supported due to failed building of ngtcp2.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Install](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/install.html)
.

Basic Usage[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#basic-usage "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### requests-like[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#requests-like "Link to this heading")

import curl\_cffi

url \= "https://tls.browserleaks.com/json"

\# Notice the impersonate parameter
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110")

print(r.json())
\# output: {..., "ja3n\_hash": "aa56c057ad164ec4fdcb7a5a283be9fc", ...}
\# the js3n fingerprint should be the same as target browser

\# To keep using the latest browser version as \`curl\_cffi\` updates,
\# simply set impersonate="chrome" without specifying a version.
\# Other similar values are: "safari" and "safari\_ios"
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome")

\# http/socks proxies are supported
proxies \= {"https": "http://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

proxies \= {"https": "socks://localhost:3128"}
r \= curl\_cffi.get("https://tls.browserleaks.com/json", impersonate\="chrome110", proxies\=proxies)

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#sessions "Link to this heading")

s \= curl\_cffi.Session()

\# httpbin is a http test website
s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies/set/foo/bar")

print(s.cookies)
\# <Cookies\[<Cookie foo=bar for httpbin.org />\]>

r \= s.get("https://httpbin.org/cookies")
print(r.json())
\# {'cookies': {'foo': 'bar'}}

### asyncio[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#asyncio "Link to this heading")

async with curl\_cffi.AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

More concurrency:

import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

urls \= \[\
    "https://google.com/",\
    "https://facebook.com/",\
    "https://twitter.com/",\
\]

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    tasks \= \[\]
    for url in urls:
        task \= s.get(url)
        tasks.append(task)
    results \= await asyncio.gather(\*tasks)

### WebSockets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.1/#websockets "Link to this heading")

from curl\_cffi import Session, WebSocket

def on\_message(ws: WebSocket, message):
    print(message)

with Session() as s:
    ws \= s.ws\_connect(
        "wss://api.gemini.com/v1/marketdata/BTCUSD",
        on\_message\=on\_message,
    )
    ws.run\_forever()

\# asyncio
import asyncio
from curl\_cffi import AsyncSession

async with AsyncSession() as s:
    ws \= await s.ws\_connect("wss://echo.websocket.org")
    await asyncio.gather(\*\[ws.send\_str("Hello, World!") for \_ in range(10)\])
    async for message in ws:
        print(message)

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### SerpAPI[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#serpapi "Link to this heading")

[![SerpAPI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/serpapi.png)](https://serpapi.com/)

Scrape Google and other search engines from [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/)
’s fast, easy, and complete API. 0.66s average response time (≤ 0.5s for Ludicrous Speed Max accounts), 99.95% SLAs, pay for successful responses only.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### Easy Captcha Bypass for Scraping[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#easy-captcha-bypass-for-scraping "Link to this heading")

[![Capsolver](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/capsolver.jpg)](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

[CapSolver](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)
 is an AI-powered tool that easily bypasses Captchas, allowing uninterrupted access to public data. It supports a variety of Captchas and works seamlessly with `curl_cffi`, Puppeteer, Playwright, and more. Fast, reliable, and cost-effective. Plus, `curl_cffi` users can use the code **“CURL”** to get an extra 6% balance! and register [here](https://dashboard.capsolver.com/passport/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#id4 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ☑️  | ☑️  |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl to make it work, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Since v0.11.0. However, only Linux and macOS are supported, Windows is not supported due to failed building of ngtcp2.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see install.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/#documentation "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/quick_start.html)
. Then the [Impersonate Guide](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/impersonate.html)
.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/api.html)
.

If you have any questions, be sure to check out the [FAQ](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.3/faq.html)
 section before opening an issue.

[Discuss on Telegram](https://t.me/+lL9n33eZp480MGM1)

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### SerpAPI[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#serpapi "Link to this heading")

[![SerpAPI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/serpapi.png)](https://serpapi.com/)

Scrape Google and other search engines from [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/)
’s fast, easy, and complete API. 0.66s average response time (≤ 0.5s for Ludicrous Speed Max accounts), 99.95% SLAs, pay for successful responses only.

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

### Easy Captcha Bypass for Scraping[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#easy-captcha-bypass-for-scraping "Link to this heading")

[![Capsolver](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/capsolver.jpg)](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

[CapSolver](https://www.capsolver.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)
 is an AI-powered tool that easily bypasses Captchas, allowing uninterrupted access to public data. It supports a variety of Captchas and works seamlessly with `curl_cffi`, Puppeteer, Playwright, and more. Fast, reliable, and cost-effective. Plus, `curl_cffi` users can use the code **“CURL”** to get an extra 6% balance! and register [here](https://dashboard.capsolver.com/passport/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=banner_repo&utm_campaign=scraping&utm_term=curl_cffi)

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#id4 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ☑️  | ☑️  |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl to make it work, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Since v0.11.0. However, only Linux and macOS are supported, Windows is not supported due to failed building of ngtcp2.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see install.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/#documentation "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/quick_start.html)
. Then the [Impersonate Guide](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/impersonate.html)
.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/api.html)
.

If you have any questions, be sure to check out the [FAQ](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.4/faq.html)
 section before opening an issue.

curl\_cffi is a Python binding for [curl-impersonate fork](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
 via [cffi](https://cffi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
. For commercial support, visit [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
.

Unlike other pure Python http clients like `httpx` or `requests`, `curl_cffi` can impersonate browsers’ TLS signatures or JA3 fingerprints. If you are blocked by some website for no obvious reason, you can give this package a try.

If you are looking for Python http3 clients, curl\_cffi added http3 support since `v0.11`.

Sponsors[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#sponsors "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bypass Cloudflare with API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#bypass-cloudflare-with-api "Link to this heading")

[![YesCaptcha](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/main/assets/yescaptcha.png)](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)

[Yescaptcha](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 is a proxy service that bypasses Cloudflare and uses the API interface to obtain verified cookies (e.g. `cf_clearance`). Click [here](https://yescaptcha.com/i/stfnIO)
 to register.

You can also click [here](https://buymeacoffee.com/yifei)
 to buy me a coffee.

Features[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#features "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Supports JA3/TLS and http2 fingerprints impersonation, including recent browsers and custom fingerprints.
    
*   Much faster than requests/httpx, on par with aiohttp/pycurl, see [benchmarks](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/benchmark)
    .
    
*   Mimics requests API, no need to learn another one.
    
*   Pre-compiled, so you don’t have to compile on your machine.
    
*   Supports `asyncio` with proxy rotation on each request.
    
*   Supports http 2.0 & 3.0, which requests does not.
    
*   Supports websocket.
    

|     |     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |Feature matrix[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#id2 "Link to this table")
      
|     | requests | aiohttp | httpx | pycurl | curl\_cffi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| http2 | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| http3 | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   |
| sync | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| async | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ✅   |
| websocket | ❌   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| fingerprints | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   |
| speed | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇  | 🐇🐇 | 🐇🐇 |

Notes:

1.  For pycurl, you need a http/3 enabled libcurl, while curl\_cffi packages libcurl-impersonate inside Python wheels.
    
2.  Full http/3 supported was added in v0.12.0.
    

Install[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#install "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pip install curl\_cffi \--upgrade

For more details, see [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/quick_start.html)
.

Documentation[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/#documentation "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can first check out [Quick Start](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/quick_start.html)
. Then the impersonate.

For advanced topics, checkout [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/cookies.html)
, [Asyncio](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/asyncio.html)
 and [WebSockets](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/websockets.html)
.

You can also find common use cases in the [examples](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl_cffi/tree/main/examples)
 directory.

Finally, if something is missing from the tutorial, you can always find them in the [API References](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.13.0/api.html)
.
