API References[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#api-references "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================

curl low levl APIs[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl-low-levl-apis "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Curl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.Curl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_*` functions of libcurl.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, certs from `certifi` are used.
    
*   **debug** – whether to show curl debug messages.
    
*   **handle** – a curl handle instance from `curl_easy_init`.
    

debug() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.debug)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.debug "Link to this definition")

Set debug to True

setopt(_option: [CurlOpt](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlOpt "curl_cffi.const.CurlOpt")
_, _value: Any_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_setopt`.

Parameters:

*   **option** – option to set, using constants from CurlOpt enum
    
*   **value** – value to set, strings will be handled automatically
    

Returns:

0 if no error, see `CurlECode`.

getinfo(_option: [CurlInfo](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlInfo "curl_cffi.const.CurlInfo")
_) → bytes | int | float | list[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.getinfo)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.getinfo "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_getinfo`. Gets information in response after curl.perform.

Parameters:

**option** – option to get info of, using constants from `CurlInfo` enum

Returns:

value retrieved from last perform.

version() → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.version)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.version "Link to this definition")

Get the underlying libcurl version.

impersonate(_target: str_, _default\_headers: bool \= True_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.impersonate)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.impersonate "Link to this definition")

Set the browser type to impersonate.

Parameters:

*   **target** – browser to impersonate.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to add default headers, like User-Agent.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

perform(_clear\_headers: bool \= True_, _clear\_resolve: bool \= True_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.perform)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.perform "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_perform`, performs a curl request.

Parameters:

*   **clear\_headers** – clear header slist used in this perform
    
*   **clear\_resolve** – clear resolve slist used in this perform
    

Raises:

**CurlError** – if the perform was not successful.

duphandle() → [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.duphandle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.duphandle "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_duphandle`.

This is not a full copy of entire curl object in python. For example, headers handle is not copied, you have to set them again.

reset() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.reset)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.reset "Link to this definition")

Reset all curl options, wrapper for `curl_easy_reset`.

parse\_cookie\_headers(_headers: list\[bytes\]_) → SimpleCookie[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_cookie_headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_cookie_headers "Link to this definition")

Extract `cookies.SimpleCookie` from header lines.

Parameters:

**headers** – list of headers in bytes.

Returns:

A parsed cookies.SimpleCookie instance.

static get\_reason\_phrase(_status\_line: bytes_) → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.get_reason_phrase)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.get_reason_phrase "Link to this definition")

Extract reason phrase, like `OK`, `Not Found` from response status line.

static parse\_status\_line(_status\_line: bytes_) → tuple\[[CurlHttpVersion](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "curl_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion")\
, int, bytes\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_status_line)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_status_line "Link to this definition")

Parse status line.

Returns:

http\_version, status\_code, and reason phrase

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup curl handle, wrapper for `curl_easy_cleanup`.

ws\_recv() → tuple\[bytes, CurlWsFrame\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_recv)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_recv "Link to this definition")

Receive a frame from a websocket connection.

Returns:

a tuple of frame content and curl frame meta struct.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_send(_payload: bytes_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")
 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_send)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send "Link to this definition")

Send data to a websocket connection.

Parameters:

*   **payload** – content to send.
    
*   **flags** – websocket flag to set for the frame, default: binary.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_close(_code: int \= 1000_, _message: bytes \= b''_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_close "Link to this definition")

Close a websocket connection. Shorthand for [`ws_send()`](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send "curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send")
 with close code and message. Note that to completely close the connection, you must close the curl handle after this call with [`close()`](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.close "curl_cffi.Curl.close")
.

Parameters:

*   **code** – close code.
    
*   **message** – close message.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

### AsyncCurl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#asynccurl "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi handle to provide asyncio support. It uses the libcurl socket\_action APIs.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, certs from `certifi` are used.
    
*   **loop** – EventLoop to use.
    

add\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.add_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.add_handle "Link to this definition")

Add a curl handle to be managed by curl\_multi. This is the equivalent of perform in the async world.

remove\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.remove_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.remove_handle "Link to this definition")

Cancel a future for given curl handle.

set\_result(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_result)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_result "Link to this definition")

Mark a future as done for given curl handle.

set\_exception(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_, _exception_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_exception)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_exception "Link to this definition")

Raise exception of a future for given curl handle.

setopt(_option_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi\_setopt.

socket\_action(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.socket_action)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.socket_action "Link to this definition")

wrapper for curl\_multi\_socket\_action, returns the number of running curl handles.

process\_data(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.process_data)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.process_data "Link to this definition")

Call curl\_multi\_info\_read to read data for given socket.

async close()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup running timers, readers, writers and handles.

### CurlMime[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curlmime "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.CurlMime(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for the `curl_mime_` API.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

**curl** – Curl instance to use.

addpart(_name: str_, _\*_, _content\_type: str | None \= None_, _filename: str | None \= None_, _local\_path: str | bytes | Path | None \= None_, _data: bytes | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.addpart)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.addpart "Link to this definition")

Add a mime part for a mutlipart html form.

Note: You can only use either local\_path or data, not both.

Parameters:

*   **name** – name of the field.
    
*   **content\_type** – content\_type for the field. for example: `image/png`.
    
*   **filename** – filename for the server.
    
*   **local\_path** – file to upload on local disk.
    
*   **data** – file content to upload.
    

classmethod from\_list(_files: list\[dict\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.from_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.from_list "Link to this definition")

Create a multipart instance from a list of dict, for keys, see `addpart`

attach(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.attach)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.attach "Link to this definition")

Attach the mime instance to a curl instance.

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.close "Link to this definition")

Close the mime instance and underlying files. This method must be called after `perform` or `request`.

### Constants[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#constants "Link to this heading")

Enum values used by `setopt` and `getinfo` can be accessed from `CurlOpt` and `CurlInfo`.

class curl\_cffi.CurlOpt(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlOpt)

`CULROPT_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_easy\_setopt.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_easy_setopt.html)

`CURLINFO_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_easy\_getinfo.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_easy_getinfo.html)

[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMOpt "Link to this definition")

`CURLMOPT_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_multi\_setopt.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_multi_setopt.html)

class curl\_cffi.CurlECode(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlECode)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlECode "Link to this definition")

class curl\_cffi.CurlHttpVersion(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlHttpVersion)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "Link to this definition")

`CURL_HTTP_VERSION` constants from libcurl, see comments for details.

class curl\_cffi.CurlWsFlag(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlWsFlag)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "Link to this definition")

`CURL_WS_FLAG` constants from libcurl, see comments for details.

requests-like API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#requests-like-api "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### request method[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#request-method "Link to this heading")

`requests.get`, `requests.post`, etc are just aliases of `.request(METHOD, ...)`

curl\_cffi.requests.request(_method: Literal\['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS', 'HEAD', 'TRACE', 'PATCH', 'QUERY'\]_, _url: str_, _thread: Literal\['eventlet', 'gevent'\] | None \= None_, _curl\_options: dict | None \= None_, _debug: bool | None \= None_, _\*\*kwargs: None_) → [Response](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "curl_cffi.requests.models.Response")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests.html#request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.request "Link to this definition")

Send an http request.

Parameters:

*   **method** – http method for the request: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE etc.
    
*   **url** – url for the requests.
    
*   **params** – query string for the requests.
    
*   **data** – form values(dict/list/tuple) or binary data to use in body, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` will be added if a dict is given.
    
*   **json** – json values to use in body, Content-Type: application/json will be added automatically.
    
*   **headers** – headers to send.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.
    
*   **files** – not supported, use `multipart` instead.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.
    
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.
    
*   **content\_callback** – a callback function to receive response body. `def callback(chunk: bytes) -> None:`
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai strings.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers when impersonating.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.
    
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.
    
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.
    
*   **debug** – print extra curl debug info.
    
*   **interface** – which interface to use.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **stream** – streaming the response, default False.
    
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.
    
*   **multipart** – upload files using the multipart format, see examples for details.
    
*   **discard\_cookies** – discard cookies from server. Default to False.
    

Returns:

A `Response` object.

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#sessions "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.requests.Session(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: ThreadType | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session "Link to this definition")

A request session, cookies and connections will be reused. This object is thread-safe, but it’s recommended to use a separate session for each thread.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: ThreadType | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the `__init__` method will be overriden by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **curl** – curl object to use in the session. If not provided, a new one will be created. Also, a fresh curl object will always be created when accessed from another thread.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use proxy if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use as base for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **interface** – which interface use.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **response\_class** – A customized subtype of `Response` to use.
    
*   **raise\_for\_status** – automatically raise an HTTPError for 4xx and 5xx status codes.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager.

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session

with Session() as s:
    r \= s.get("https://example.com")

list\[tuple\] | str | ~io.BytesIO | bytes | None \= None, json: dict | list | None \= None, headers: ~curl\_cffi.requests.headers.Headers | ~collections.abc.Mapping\[str, str | None\] | ~typing.Literal\[False\] \= '', http\_version: ~curl\_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion | ~typing.Literal\['v1', 'v2', 'v2tls', 'v2\_prior\_knowledge', 'v3', 'v3only'\] | None \= None, interface: str | None \= None, cert: str | tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None, stream: bool | None \= None, max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0, multipart: ~curl\_cffi.curl.CurlMime | None \= None, discard\_cookies: bool \= False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.request "Link to this definition")

Send the request, see `requests.request` for details on parameters.

stream(_method: Literal\['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS', 'HEAD', 'TRACE', 'PATCH', 'QUERY'\]_, _url: str_, _\*\*kwargs: None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.stream)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.stream "Link to this definition")

Equivalent to `with request(..., stream=True) as r:`

ws\_connect(_url_, _on\_message\=None_, _on\_error\=None_, _on\_open\=None_, _on\_close\=None_, _\*\*kwargs_) → [WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

Connects to a websocket url.

Note: This method is deprecated, use WebSocket instead.

Parameters:

*   **url** – the ws url to connect.
    
*   **on\_message** – message callback, `def on_message(ws, str)`
    
*   **on\_error** – error callback, `def on_error(ws, error)`
    
*   **on\_open** – open callback, `def on_open(ws)`
    
*   **on\_close** – close callback, `def on_close(ws)`
    

Other parameters are the same as `.request`

Returns:

a WebSocket instance to communicate with the server.

class curl\_cffi.requests.AsyncSession(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "Link to this definition")

An async request session, cookies and connections will be reused.

\_\_init\_\_(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the `__init__` method will be override by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **loop** – loop to use, if not provided, the running loop will be used.
    
*   **async\_curl** – \[AsyncCurl\](/api/curl\_cffi#curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl) object to use.
    
*   **max\_clients** – maxmium curl handle to use in the session, this will affect the concurrency ratio.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **response\_class** – A customized subtype of `Response` to use.
    
*   **raise\_for\_status** – automatically raise an HTTPError for 4xx and 5xx status codes.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager, and it’s recommended to use via `async with`. However, unlike aiohttp, it is not required to use `with`.

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

\# recommended.
async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

s \= AsyncSession()  \# it also works.

_max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0_, _recv\_queue\_size: int \= 512_, _send\_queue\_size: int \= 256_, _max\_send\_batch\_size: int \= 256_, _coalesce\_frames: bool \= False_, _retry\_on\_recv\_error: bool \= False_, _yield\_interval: float \= 0.001_, _fair\_scheduling: bool \= False_, _yield\_mask: int \= 63_) → [AsyncWebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.AsyncWebSocket")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

Connects to a WebSocket.

Parameters:

*   **url** – url for the requests.
    
*   **autoclose** – whether to close the WebSocket after receiving a close frame.
    
*   **params** – query string for the requests.
    
*   **headers** – headers to send.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.
    
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers.
    
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.
    
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.
    
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.
    
*   **interface** – which interface to use.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.
    
*   **recv\_queue\_size** – The maximum number of incoming WebSocket messages to buffer internally. This queue stores messages received by the Curl socket that are waiting to be consumed by calling recv().
    
*   **send\_queue\_size** – The maximum number of outgoing WebSocket messages to buffer before applying network backpressure. When you call send(…) the message is placed in this queue and transmitted when the Curl socket is next available for sending.
    
*   **max\_send\_batch\_size** – The max batch size for sent frames.
    
*   **coalesce\_frames** – If True, multiple pending messages in the send queue may be merged into a single WebSocket frame for improved throughput. **Warning:** This breaks the one-to-one mapping of send() calls to frames and should only be used when the application protocol is designed to handle concatenated data streams. Defaults to False.
    
*   **retry\_on\_recv\_error** – Retries ws\_recv() if a recv error is raised. Retries up to a limited number of times with a delay in between.
    
*   **yield\_interval** – How often to yield control back to the event loop. This is a trade-off between throughput and responsiveness. Lower values means the loop yields more frequently and enables other tasks to run, while higher values are better for throughput. The balanced default is 1ms but you can customize this to fit your application/use case.
    
*   **fair\_scheduling** – Changes the I/O priority from favoring receives (5:1) to a balanced ratio (1:1). Enable this to improve send responsiveness under heavy, concurrent load, at the cost of significantly lower overall throughput.
    
*   **yield\_mask** – Controls the frequency of cooperative multitasking yields in the read loop. The loop yields every yield\_mask + 1 operations. For efficiency, this value must be a power of two minus one (e.g., 63, 127, 255). Lower values yield more often, improving fairness at the cost of throughput. Higher values yield less often, prioritizing throughput.
    

### Headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#headers "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.requests.Headers(_headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
 | Mapping\[str, str | None\] | Mapping\[bytes, bytes | None\] | Sequence\[tuple\[str, str\]\] | Sequence\[tuple\[bytes, bytes\]\] | Sequence\[str | bytes\] | None \= None_, _encoding: str | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "Link to this definition")

HTTP headers, as a case-insensitive multi-dict.

property encoding: str[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.encoding "Link to this definition")

Header encoding is mandated as ascii, but we allow fallbacks to utf-8 or iso-8859-1.

raw()[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.raw "Link to this definition")

Returns a list of the raw header items, as byte pairs.

multi\_items() → list\[tuple\[str, str | None\]\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.multi_items)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.multi_items "Link to this definition")

Return a list of (key, value) pairs of headers. Allow multiple occurrences of the same key without concatenating into a single comma separated value.

get(_key: str_, _default: Any \= None_) → Any[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get "Link to this definition")

Return a header value. If multiple occurrences of the header occur then concatenate them together with commas.

get\_list(_key: str_, _split\_commas: bool \= False_) → list\[str | None\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get_list "Link to this definition")

Return a list of all header values for a given key. If split\_commas=True is passed, then any comma separated header values are split into multiple return strings.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_key: str_) → str | None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

Return a single header value. If there are multiple headers with the same key, then we concatenate them with commas. See: [https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2)

\_\_setitem\_\_(_key: str_, _value: str | None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

Set the header key to value, removing any duplicate entries. Retains insertion order.

\_\_delitem\_\_(_key: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

Remove the header key.

### Cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#cookies "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.requests.Cookies(_cookies: [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "curl_cffi.requests.cookies.Cookies")
 | CookieJar | dict\[str, str\] | list\[tuple\[str, str\]\] | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "Link to this definition")

HTTP Cookies, as a mutable mapping.

set(_name: str_, _value: str_, _domain: str \= ''_, _path: str \= '/'_, _secure\=False_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.set)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.set "Link to this definition")

Set a cookie value by name. May optionally include domain and path.

get(_name: str_, _default: str | None \= None_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → str | None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.get "Link to this definition")

Get a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to retrieve.

delete(_name: str_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.delete)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.delete "Link to this definition")

Delete a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to delete.

clear(_domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.clear)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.clear "Link to this definition")

Delete all cookies. Optionally include a domain and path in order to only delete a subset of all the cookies.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_name: str_) → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_setitem\_\_(_name: str_, _value: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_delitem\_\_(_name: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

### Request, Response[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#request-response "Link to this heading")

class curl\_cffi.requests.Request(_url: str_, _headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
_, _method: str_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "Link to this definition")

Representing a sent request.

class curl\_cffi.requests.Response(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _request: [Request](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "curl_cffi.requests.models.Request")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "Link to this definition")

Contains information the server sends.

url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.url "Link to this definition")

url used in the request.

content[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.content "Link to this definition")

response body in bytes.

text[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.text "Link to this definition")

response body in str.

status\_code[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.status_code "Link to this definition")

http status code.

reason[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.reason "Link to this definition")

http response reason, such as OK, Not Found.

ok[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.ok "Link to this definition")

is status\_code in \[200, 400)?\
\
headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.headers "Link to this definition")\
\
response headers.\
\
cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.cookies "Link to this definition")\
\
response cookies.\
\
elapsed[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.elapsed "Link to this definition")\
\
timedelta of the request duration.\
\
encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
http body encoding.\
\
charset[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset "Link to this definition")\
\
alias for encoding.\
\
primary\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.primary_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
primary ip of the server.\
\
primary\_port[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.primary_port "Link to this definition")\
\
primary port of the server.\
\
local\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.local_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
local ip used in this connection.\
\
local\_port[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.local_port "Link to this definition")\
\
local port used in this connection.\
\
charset\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding specified by the Content-Type header.\
\
default\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.default_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.\
\
redirect\_count[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_count "Link to this definition")\
\
how many redirects happened.\
\
redirect\_url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_url "Link to this definition")\
\
the final redirected url.\
\
http\_version[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.http_version "Link to this definition")\
\
http version used.\
\
history[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.history "Link to this definition")\
\
history redirections, only headers are available.\
\
download\_size[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.download_size "Link to this definition")\
\
total downloaded bytes (body).\
\
upload\_size[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.upload_size "Link to this definition")\
\
total uploaded bytes (body).\
\
header\_size[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.header_size "Link to this definition")\
\
total header size.\
\
request\_size[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.request_size "Link to this definition")\
\
request size.\
\
response\_size[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.response_size "Link to this definition")\
\
download\_size + header\_size\
\
raise\_for\_status()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.raise_for_status)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.raise_for_status "Link to this definition")\
\
Raise an error if status code is not in \[200, 400)\
\
iter\_lines(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_, _delimiter\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_lines)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_lines "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content line by line, separated by `\n`.\

 which is under the License: Apache 2.0\
\
iter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_content)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_content "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
json(_\*\*kw_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.json)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.json "Link to this definition")\
\
return a parsed json object of the content.\
\
close()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.close)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.close "Link to this definition")\
\
Close the streaming connection, only valid in stream mode.\
\
async aiter\_lines(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_, _delimiter\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.aiter_lines)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.aiter_lines "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content line by line, separated by `\n`.\
\
Copied from: [https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/\_modules/requests/models/](https://requests.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/requests/models/)\
 which is under the License: Apache 2.0\
\
async aiter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.aiter_content)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.aiter_content "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
async atext() → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.atext)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.atext "Link to this definition")\
\
Return a decoded string.\
\
async acontent() → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.acontent)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.acontent "Link to this definition")\
\
wait and read the streaming content in one bytes object.\
\

\
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\
WebSocket[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#websocket "Link to this heading")\
\
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\
class curl\_cffi.requests.WebSocket(_curl: Union\[Curl_, _Any\]\=<object object>_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _skip\_utf8\_validation: bool \= False_, _debug: bool \= False_, _on\_open: ON\_OPEN\_T | None \= None_, _on\_close: ON\_CLOSE\_T | None \= None_, _on\_data: ON\_DATA\_T | None \= None_, _on\_message: ON\_MESSAGE\_T | None \= None_, _on\_error: ON\_ERROR\_T | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "Link to this definition")\
\
A WebSocket implementation using libcurl.\
\
\_\_init\_\_(_curl: Union\[Curl_, _Any\]\=<object object>_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _skip\_utf8\_validation: bool \= False_, _debug: bool \= False_, _on\_open: ON\_OPEN\_T | None \= None_, _on\_close: ON\_CLOSE\_T | None \= None_, _on\_data: ON\_DATA\_T | None \= None_, _on\_message: ON\_MESSAGE\_T | None \= None_, _on\_error: ON\_ERROR\_T | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.__init__)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.__init__ "Link to this definition")\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **autoclose** – whether to close the WebSocket after receiving a close frame.\
    \
*   **skip\_utf8\_validation** – whether to skip UTF-8 validation for text frames in run\_forever().\
    \
*   **debug** – print extra curl debug info.\
    \
*   **on\_open** – open callback, `def on_open(ws)`\
    \
*   **on\_close** – close callback, `def on_close(ws, code, reason)`\
    \
*   **on\_data** – raw data receive callback, `def on_data(ws, data, frame)`\
    \
*   **on\_message** – message receive callback, `def on_message(ws, message)`\
    \
*   **on\_error** – error callback, `def on_error(ws, exception)`\
    \
\
connect(_url: str, params: Optional\[Union\[dict, list, tuple\]\] \= None, headers: Optional\[HeaderTypes\] \= None, cookies: Optional\[CookieTypes\] \= None, auth: Optional\[tuple\[str, str\]\] \= None, timeout: Optional\[Union\[float, tuple\[float, float\], object\]\] \= <object object>, allow\_redirects: bool \= True, max\_redirects: int \= 30, proxies: Optional\[ProxySpec\] \= None, proxy: Optional\[str\] \= None, proxy\_auth: Optional\[tuple\[str, str\]\] \= None, verify: Optional\[bool\] \= None, referer: Optional\[str\] \= None, accept\_encoding: Optional\[str\] \= 'gzip, deflate, br', impersonate: Optional\[BrowserTypeLiteral\] \= None, ja3: Optional\[str\] \= None, akamai: Optional\[str\] \= None, extra\_fp: Optional\[Union\[ExtraFingerprints, ExtraFpDict\]\] \= None, default\_headers: bool \= True, quote: Union\[str, Literal\[False\]\] \= '', http\_version: Optional\[CurlHttpVersion\] \= None, interface: Optional\[str\] \= None, cert: Optional\[Union\[str, tuple\[str, str\]\]\] \= None, max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0, curl\_options: Optional\[dict\[CurlOpt, str\]\] \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.connect)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.connect "Link to this definition")\
\
Connect to the WebSocket.\
\
libcurl automatically handles pings and pongs. ref: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html)\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **url** – url for the requests.\
    \
*   **params** – query string for the requests.\
    \
*   **headers** – headers to send.\
    \
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.\
    \
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.\
    \
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.\
    \
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.\
    \
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.\
    \
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.\
    \
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)\
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.\
    \
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).\
    \
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.\
    \
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.\
    \
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.\
    \
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.\
    \
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.\
    \
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.\
    \
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.\
    \
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers.\
    \
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.\
    \
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.\
    \
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.\
    \
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.\
    \
*   **interface** – which interface to use.\
    \
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.\
    \
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.\
    \
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.\
    \
\
recv\_fragment() → tuple\[bytes, CurlWsFrame\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv_fragment)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv_fragment "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a single curl websocket fragment as bytes.\
\
recv() → tuple\[bytes, int\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a frame as bytes. libcurl splits frames into fragments, so we have to collect all the chunks for a frame.\
\
recv\_str() → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv_str)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv_str "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a text frame.\
\
recv\_json(_\*, loads: Callable\[\[str\], T\] \= <function loads>_) → T[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv_json)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv_json "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a JSON frame.\
\
Parameters:\
\

class curl\_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket(_session: [AsyncSession](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession")\
_, _curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")\
_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _debug: bool \= False_, _recv\_queue\_size: int \= 512_, _send\_queue\_size: int \= 256_, _max\_send\_batch\_size: int \= 256_, _coalesce\_frames: bool \= False_, _retry\_on\_recv\_error: bool \= False_, _yield\_interval: float \= 0.001_, _fair\_scheduling: bool \= False_, _yield\_mask: int \= 63_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket "Link to this definition")\
\
An asyncio WebSocket implementation using libcurl.\
\
NOTE: This object represents a single WebSocket connection. Once closed, it cannot be reopened. A new instance must be created to reconnect.\
\
\_\_init\_\_(_session: [AsyncSession](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession")\
_, _curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")\
_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _debug: bool \= False_, _recv\_queue\_size: int \= 512_, _send\_queue\_size: int \= 256_, _max\_send\_batch\_size: int \= 256_, _coalesce\_frames: bool \= False_, _retry\_on\_recv\_error: bool \= False_, _yield\_interval: float \= 0.001_, _fair\_scheduling: bool \= False_, _yield\_mask: int \= 63_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.__init__)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.__init__ "Link to this definition")\
\
Initializes an Async WebSocket session.\
\
This class should not be instantiated directly. It is intended to be created via the AsyncSession.ws\_connect() method, which correctly handles setup and initialization of the underlying I/O tasks.\
\
Important\
\
This WebSocket implementation uses a decoupled I/O model. Network operations occur in background tasks. As a result, network-related errors that occur during a send() operation will not be raised by send(). Instead, they are placed into the receive queue and will be raised by the next call to recv().\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **session** ([_AsyncSession_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession")\
    ) – An instantiated AsyncSession object.\
    \
*   **curl** ([_Curl_](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")\
    ) – The underlying Curl to use.\
    \
*   **autoclose** (_bool__,_ _optional_) – Close the WS on receiving a close frame.\
    \
*   **debug** (_bool__,_ _optional_) – Enable debug messages. Defaults to False.\
    \
*   **recv\_queue\_size** (_int__,_ _optional_) – The maximum number of incoming WebSocket messages to buffer internally. This queue stores messages received by the Curl socket that are waiting to be consumed by calling recv()\
    \
*   **send\_queue\_size** (_int__,_ _optional_) – The maximum number of outgoing WebSocket messages to buffer before applying network backpressure. When you call send(…) the message is placed in this queue and transmitted when the Curl socket is next available for sending.\
    \
*   **max\_send\_batch\_size** (_int__,_ _optional_) – The max number of messages per batch.\
    \
*   **coalesce\_frames** (_bool__,_ _optional_) – Combine multiple frames into a batch.\
    \
*   **retry\_on\_recv\_error** (_bool__,_ _optional_) – Retry recv on some transient errors.\
    \
*   **yield\_interval** (_float__,_ _optional_) – How often to yield control in seconds.\
    \
*   **fair\_scheduling** (_bool__,_ _optional_) – Change the ~5:1 ratio in favor of recv:send to a fairer 1:1 ratio. This decreases recv throughput.\
    \
*   **yield\_mask** (_int__,_ _optional_) – A bitmask that sets the yield frequency for cooperative multitasking, checked every yield\_mask + 1 operations. Must be a power of two minus one (e.g., 63, 127, 255) for efficient bitwise checks. Lower values increase fairness; higher values increase throughput.\
    \
\
async recv(_\*_, _timeout: float | None \= None_) → tuple\[bytes | None, int\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.recv)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.recv "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a frame as bytes.\
\
This method waits for and returns the next complete data frame from the receive queue.\
\
Parameters:\
\
**timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.\
\
Raises:\
\
*   **WebSocketClosed** – If recv() is called on a closed connection after the receive queue is empty.\
    \
*   **WebSocketTimeout** – If the operation times out.\
    \
*   **WebSocketError** – A protocol or network error that occurred in a background I/O task, including errors from previous send() operations.\
    \
\
Returns:\
\
A tuple with the received payload and flags.\
\
Return type:\
\
tuple\[bytes, int\]\
\
async recv\_str(_\*_, _timeout: float | None \= None_) → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.recv_str)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.recv_str "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a text frame.\
\
Parameters:\
\
**timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.\
\
async recv\_json(_\*, loads: Callable\[\[Union\[str, bytes\]\], T\] \= <function loads>, timeout: Optional\[float\] \= None_) → T[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.recv_json)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.recv_json "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a JSON frame.\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **loads** – JSON decoder, default is json.loads.\
    \
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.\
    \
\
async send(_payload: str | bytes | bytearray | memoryview_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")\
 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.send)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.send "Link to this definition")\
\
Send a data frame.\
\
This method is a lightweight, non-blocking call that places the payload into a send queue. The actual network transmission is handled by a background task.\
\
To guarantee all your messages have been sent await ws.flush(…).\
\
The max frame size supported by libcurl is 65535 bytes. Larger frames will be broken down and sent in chunks of that size.\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **payload** – data to send.\
    \
*   **flags** – flags for the frame.\
    \
\
Raises:\
\
**WebSocketClosed** – The WebSocket is closed.\
\
Note\
\
Due to the asynchronous nature of this client, network errors (e.g., connection dropped) that occur during the actual transmission will NOT be raised by this method. They will be raised by a subsequent call to recv(). Always ensure you are actively receiving data to handle potential connection errors.\
\
Also: If the network is slow and the internal send queue becomes full, this method will block until there is space in the queue.\
\
async send\_binary(_payload: bytes_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.send_binary)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.send_binary "Link to this definition")\

async close(_code: int \= WsCloseCode.OK_, _message: bytes \= b''_, _timeout: float \= 5.0_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#AsyncWebSocket.close)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncWebSocket.close "Link to this definition")\
\
Performs a graceful WebSocket closing handshake and terminates the connection.\
\
This method sends a WebSocket close frame to the peer, waits for queued outgoing messages to be sent, and then shuts down the connection. This is the recommended way to close the session.\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **code** (_int__,_ _optional_) – Close code. Defaults to WsCloseCode.OK.\
    \
*   **message** (_bytes__,_ _optional_) – Close reason. Defaults to b””.\
    \
*   **timeout** (_float__,_ _optional_) – How long in seconds to wait closed.\
    \
\
Exceptions and Warnings[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#exceptions-and-warnings "Link to this heading")\
\
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\
\
### Exceptions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#exceptions "Link to this heading")\
\
We try to follow the requests exception hirearchy, however, some are missing, while some are added.\
\
If an exception is marked as “not used”, please catch the base exception.\
\

class curl\_cffi.requests.exceptions.ConnectTimeout(_msg_, _code: [CurlECode](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlECode "curl_cffi.const.CurlECode")\
 | Literal\[0\] \= 0_, _response\=None_, _\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/exceptions.html#ConnectTimeout)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.exceptions.ConnectTimeout "Link to this definition")\
\
The request timed out while trying to connect to the remote server.\
\
Requests that produced this error are safe to retry.\
\
not used\
\
class curl\_cffi.requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout(_msg_, _code: [CurlECode](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlECode "curl_cffi.const.CurlECode")\

\
The server did not send any data in the allotted amount of time. not used\
\

\

Supported browser impersonate targets[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/targets.html#supported-browser-impersonate-targets "Link to this heading")

============================================================================================================================================================================

`curl_cffi` supports the same browser versions as supported by our [fork of curl-impersonate](https://github.com/lexiforest/curl-impersonate)
.

Browser versions will be added **only** when their fingerprints change. If you see a version, e.g. `chrome122`, was skipped, you can simply impersonate it with your own headers and the previous version.

If you are too busy to look up those details, you can try our commercial version at [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
, which has a weekly updated list of browser profiles and even more browser types.

If you are trying to impersonate a target other than a browser, use `ja3=...`, `akamai=...` and `extra_fp=...` to specify your own customized fingerprints. See below for details.

*   chrome99
    
*   chrome100
    
*   chrome101
    
*   chrome104
    
*   chrome107
    
*   chrome110
    
*   chrome116 1
    
*   chrome119 1
    
*   chrome120 1
    
*   chrome123 3
    
*   chrome124 3
    
*   chrome131 4
    
*   chrome133a 5 6
    
*   chrome136 7
    
*   chrome99\_android
    
*   chrome131\_android 4
    
*   edge99
    
*   edge101
    
*   safari153 2
    
*   safari155 2
    
*   safari170 1
    
*   safari172\_ios 1
    
*   safari180 4
    
*   safari180\_ios 4
    
*   safari184 7
    
*   safari184\_ios 7
    
*   safari260 8
    
*   safari260\_ios 8
    
*   firefox133 5
    
*   tor145 7
    

Notes:

1.  Added in version `0.6.0`.
    
2.  Fixed in version `0.6.0`, previous http2 fingerprints were [not correct](https://github.com/lwthiker/curl-impersonate/issues/215)
    .
    
3.  Added in version `0.7.0`.
    
4.  Added in version `0.8.0`.
    
5.  Added in version `0.9.0`.
    
6.  The version postfix `-a``(e.g. ``chrome133a`) means that this is an alternative version, i.e. the fingerprint has not been officially updated by browser, but has been observed because of A/B testing.
    
7.  Added in version `0.11.0`
    
8.  Added in version `0.12.0`
    

Which target version to use?[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/targets.html#which-target-version-to-use "Link to this heading")

=========================================================================================================================================================

Generally speaking, you should use the latest Chrome or Safari versions. As of v0.11, they’re `chrome136`, `safari184` and `safari184_ios`. To always impersonate the latest available browser versions, you can simply use `chrome`, `firefox`, `safari` and `chrome_android`, `safari_ios`.

import curl\_cffi

curl\_cffi.get(url, impersonate\="chrome")

Tips:

iOS has restrictions on WebView and TLS libs, so `safari_*_ios` should work for a lot of apps. If you encountered an android app with custom fingerprints, you can try the `safari_ios` fingerprints, given that this app should have an iOS version.

# What is TLS and http/2, http/3 fingerprinting? — curl_cffi  documentation

### Navigation

*   [index](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/genindex.html "General Index")
    
*   [next](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/targets.html "Supported browser impersonate targets")
     |
*   [previous](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/_index.html "Impersonate Guide")
     |
*   [curl\_cffi documentation](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/index.html)
     »
*   [Impersonate Guide](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/_index.html)
     »
*   [What is TLS and http/2, http/3 fingerprinting?](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html)
    

What is TLS and http/2, http/3 fingerprinting?[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html#what-is-tls-and-http-2-http-3-fingerprinting "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================================================================================================

TLS and http/2[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html#tls-and-http-2 "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TLS is the `s` in `https`. `https` has been uniformly deployed across the world. There are many extension and cipher suites a implementation can choose to use. According to the RFC, there are many valid combinations. But in reality, browser vendors tend to use fixed combinations, and these combinations can be used identify if the request is from a certain browser or an automated script. The digest of this combination is called a TLS fingerprints. The most common digesting method is called JA3.

Similar to TLS, there are a few settings in http/2 connection can be used to identify the source of a request. The most commonly used digesting method is proposed by Akamai, and called the Akamai http2 fingerprint.

To learn the details of TLS and http2 fingerprinting, you can read these great articles from lwthiker:

1.  [https://lwthiker.com/networks/2022/06/17/tls-fingerprinting.html](https://lwthiker.com/networks/2022/06/17/tls-fingerprinting.html)
    
2.  [https://lwthiker.com/networks/2022/06/17/http2-fingerprinting.html](https://lwthiker.com/networks/2022/06/17/http2-fingerprinting.html)
    

The format of JA3 and Akamai digest is briefly discussed below.

http/3[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html#http-3 "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As of http/3, the newest version of http. Basically, it’s http/2 reimplemented over QUIC, thus it can be fingerprinted in a similar way with http/2.

Http3 fingerprints has not yet been publicly exploited and reported. But given the rapidly increasing marketshare of http/3(35% of internet traffic), it is expected that some strict WAF vendors have begun to utilize http/3 fingerprinting.

It has also been noticed by many users, that, for a lot of sites, there is less or even none detection when using http/3.

`curl_cffi` provides TLS and http/2 impersonation in the open source version.

For http/3 impersonation and http/3 proxy support, please head over to [impersonate.pro](https://impersonate.pro/)
 for the commercial version of `curl_cffi`.

    *   [TLS and http/2](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html#tls-and-http-2)
        
    *   [http/3](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/fingerprint.html#http-3)

Impersonate Guide[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.14.0/impersonate/_index.html#impersonate-guide "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================================

You probably come across `curl_cffi` for it’s ability to impersonate browsers. Here is a tutorial on how to better impersonate using `curl_cffi`.
