, bytes\], None\] | None \= None_, _on\_error: Callable\[\[[WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")\
, CurlError\], None\] | None \= None_, _on\_open: Callable\[\[[WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")\
\], None\] | None \= None_, _on\_close: Callable\[\[[WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")\
, int, str\], None\] | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.__init__)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.__init__ "Link to this definition")\
\
recv() → Tuple\[bytes, int\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv "Link to this definition")\
\
Receive a frame as bytes.\
\
libcurl split frames into fragments, so we have to collect all the chunks for a frame.\
\
send(_payload: bytes_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")\
 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.send)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.send "Link to this definition")\
\
Send a data frame\
\
run\_forever()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.run_forever)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.run_forever "Link to this definition")\
\
libcurl automatically handles pings and pongs.\
\
ref: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html)\
\
close(_code: int \= WsCloseCode.OK_, _message: bytes \= b''_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.close)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.2/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.close "Link to this definition")\
\

\

# API References — curl_cffi  documentation

API References[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#api-references "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================

curl low levl APIs[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl-low-levl-apis "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Curl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.Curl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_*` functions of libcurl.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, curl\_cffi uses certs from `certifi`.
    
*   **debug** – whether to show curl debug messages.
    
*   **handle** – a curl handle instance from `curl_easy_init`.
    

debug() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.debug)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.debug "Link to this definition")

Set debug to True

setopt(_option: [CurlOpt](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlOpt "curl_cffi.const.CurlOpt")
_, _value: Any_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_setopt`.

Parameters:

*   **option** – option to set, using constants from CurlOpt enum
    
*   **value** – value to set, strings will be handled automatically
    

Returns:

0 if no error, see `CurlECode`.

getinfo(_option: [CurlInfo](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlInfo "curl_cffi.const.CurlInfo")
_) → bytes | int | float | List[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.getinfo)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.getinfo "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_getinfo`. Gets information in response after curl perform.

Parameters:

**option** – option to get info of, using constants from `CurlInfo` enum

Returns:

value retrieved from last perform.

version() → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.version)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.version "Link to this definition")

Get the underlying libcurl version.

impersonate(_target: str_, _default\_headers: bool \= True_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.impersonate)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.impersonate "Link to this definition")

Set the browser type to impersonate.

Parameters:

*   **target** – browser to impersonate.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to add default headers, like User-Agent.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

perform(_clear\_headers: bool \= True_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.perform)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.perform "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_perform`, performs a curl request.

Parameters:

**clear\_headers** – clear header slist used in this perform

Raises:

**CurlError** – if the perform was not successful.

duphandle() → [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.duphandle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.duphandle "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_duphandle`.

This is not a full copy of entire curl object in python. For example, headers handle is not copied, you have to set them again.

reset() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.reset)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.reset "Link to this definition")

Reset all curl options, wrapper for `curl_easy_reset`.

parse\_cookie\_headers(_headers: List\[bytes\]_) → SimpleCookie[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_cookie_headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_cookie_headers "Link to this definition")

Extract `cookies.SimpleCookie` from header lines.

Parameters:

**headers** – list of headers in bytes.

Returns:

A parsed cookies.SimpleCookie instance.

_static_ get\_reason\_phrase(_status\_line: bytes_) → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.get_reason_phrase)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.get_reason_phrase "Link to this definition")

Extract reason phrase, like `OK`, `Not Found` from response status line.

_static_ parse\_status\_line(_status\_line: bytes_) → Tuple\[[CurlHttpVersion](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "curl_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion")\
, int, bytes\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_status_line)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_status_line "Link to this definition")

Parse status line.

Returns:

http\_version, status\_code, and reason phrase

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup curl handle, wrapper for `curl_easy_cleanup`.

ws\_recv(_n: int \= 1024_) → Tuple\[bytes, Any\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_recv)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_recv "Link to this definition")

Receive a frame from a websocket connection.

Parameters:

**n** – maximum data to receive.

Returns:

a tuple of frame content and curl frame meta struct.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_send(_payload: bytes_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")
 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_send)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send "Link to this definition")

Send data to a websocket connection.

Parameters:

*   **payload** – content to send.
    
*   **flags** – websocket flag to set for the frame, default: binary.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_close "Link to this definition")

Send the close frame.

### AsyncCurl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#asynccurl "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi handle to provide asyncio support. It uses the libcurl socket\_action APIs.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, curl\_cffi uses certs from `certifi`.
    
*   **loop** – EventLoop to use.
    

add\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.add_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.add_handle "Link to this definition")

Add a curl handle to be managed by curl\_multi. This is the equivalent of perform in the async world.

remove\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.remove_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.remove_handle "Link to this definition")

Cancel a future for given curl handle.

set\_result(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_result)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_result "Link to this definition")

Mark a future as done for given curl handle.

set\_exception(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_, _exception_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_exception)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_exception "Link to this definition")

Raise exception of a future for given curl handle.

setopt(_option_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi\_setopt.

socket\_action(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.socket_action)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.socket_action "Link to this definition")

Call libcurl socket\_action function

process\_data(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.process_data)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.process_data "Link to this definition")

Call curl\_multi\_info\_read to read data for given socket.

_async_ close()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup running timers, readers, writers and handles.

### CurlMime[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curlmime "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlMime(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for the `curl_mime_` API.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

**curl** – Curl instance to use.

addpart(_name: str_, _\*_, _content\_type: str | None \= None_, _filename: str | None \= None_, _local\_path: str | bytes | Path | None \= None_, _data: bytes | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.addpart)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.addpart "Link to this definition")

Add a mime part for a mutlipart html form.

Note: You can only use either local\_path or data, not both.

Parameters:

*   **name** – name of the field.
    
*   **content\_type** – content\_type for the field. for example: `image/png`.
    
*   **filename** – filename for the server.
    
*   **local\_path** – file to upload on local disk.
    
*   **data** – file content to upload.
    

_classmethod_ from\_list(_files: List\[dict\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.from_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.from_list "Link to this definition")

Create a multipart instance from a list of dict, for keys, see `addpart`

attach(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.attach)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.attach "Link to this definition")

Attach the mime instance to a curl instance.

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.close "Link to this definition")

Close the mime instance and underlying files. This method must be called after `perform` or `request`.

### Constants[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#constants "Link to this heading")

Enum values used by `setopt` and `getinfo` can be accessed from `CurlOpt` and `CurlInfo`.

requests API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#requests-api "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### request method[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#request-method "Link to this heading")

`requests.get`, `requests.post`, etc are just aliases of `.request(METHOD, ...)`

curl\_cffi.requests.request(_method: str_, _url: str_, _params: Dict | List | Tuple | None \= None_, _data: Dict\[str, str\] | List\[Tuple\] | str | BytesIO | bytes | None \= None_, _json: dict | None \= None_, _headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
 | Mapping\[str, str | None\] | Mapping\[bytes, bytes | None\] | Sequence\[Tuple\[str, str\]\] | Sequence\[Tuple\[bytes, bytes\]\] | Sequence\[str | bytes\] | None \= None_, _cookies: [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "curl_cffi.requests.cookies.Cookies")
 | CookieJar | Dict\[str, str\] | List\[Tuple\[str, str\]\] | None \= None_, _files: Dict | None \= None_, _auth: Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None_, _timeout: float | Tuple\[float, float\] \= 30_, _allow\_redirects: bool \= True_, _max\_redirects: int \= 30_, _proxies: Dict\[str, str\] | None \= None_, _proxy: str | None \= None_, _proxy\_auth: Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None_, _verify: bool | None \= None_, _referer: str | None \= None_, _accept\_encoding: str | None \= 'gzip, deflate, br, zstd'_, _content\_callback: Callable | None \= None_, _impersonate: str | BrowserType | None \= None_, _ja3: str | None \= None_, _akamai: str | None \= None_, _extra\_fp: ExtraFingerprints | ExtraFpDict | None \= None_, _thread: Literal\['eventlet', 'gevent'\] | None \= None_, _default\_headers: bool | None \= None_, _default\_encoding: str | Callable\[\[bytes\], str\] \= 'utf-8'_, _curl\_options: dict | None \= None_, _http\_version: [CurlHttpVersion](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "curl_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion")
 | None \= None_, _debug: bool \= False_, _interface: str | None \= None_, _cert: str | Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None_, _stream: bool \= False_, _max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0_, _multipart: [CurlMime](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime "curl_cffi.curl.CurlMime")
 | None \= None_) → [Response](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "curl_cffi.requests.models.Response")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests.html#request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.request "Link to this definition")

Send an http request.

Parameters:

*   **method** – http method for the request: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE etc.
    
*   **url** – url for the requests.
    
*   **params** – query string for the requests.
    
*   **data** – form values(dict/list/tuple) or binary data to use in body, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` will be added if a dict is given.
    
*   **json** – json values to use in body, Content-Type: application/json will be added automatically.
    
*   **headers** – headers to send.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.
    
*   **files** – not supported, use `multipart` instead.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.
    
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.
    
*   **content\_callback** – a callback function to receive response body. `def callback(chunk: bytes) -> None:`
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai strings.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers when impersonating.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.
    
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.
    
*   **debug** – print extra curl debug info.
    
*   **interface** – which interface to use.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **stream** – streaming the response, default False.
    
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.
    
*   **multipart** – upload files using the multipart format, see examples for details.
    

Returns:

A `Response` object.

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#sessions "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Session(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: Literal\['eventlet', 'gevent'\] | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session "Link to this definition")

A request session, cookies and connections will be reused. This object is thread-safe, but it’s recommended to use a seperate session for each thread.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: Literal\['eventlet', 'gevent'\] | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the init method will be override by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **curl** – curl object to use in the session. If not provided, a new one will be created. Also, a fresh curl object will always be created when accessed from another thread.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, format: {“http”: proxy\_url, “https”: proxy\_url}.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use as base for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai strings.
    
*   **interface** – which interface use.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager.

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session

with Session() as s:
    r \= s.get("https://example.com")

request(_method: str, url: str, params: ~typing.Dict | ~typing.List | ~typing.Tuple | None \= None, data: ~typing.Dict\[str, str\] | ~typing.List\[~typing.Tuple\] | str | ~\_io.BytesIO | bytes | None \= None, json: dict | None \= None, headers: ~curl\_cffi.requests.headers.Headers | ~collections.abc.Mapping\[str, str | None\] | ~collections.abc.Mapping\[bytes, bytes | None\] | ~typing.Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None, timeout: float | ~typing.Tuple\[float, float\] | object | None \= <object object>, allow\_redirects: bool | None \= None, max\_redirects: int | None \= None, proxies: ~typing.Dict\[str, str\] | None \= None, proxy: str | None \= None, proxy\_auth: ~typing.Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None, verify: bool | None \= None, referer: str | None \= None, accept\_encoding: str | None \= 'gzip, deflate, br', content\_callback: ~typing.Callable | None \= None, impersonate: str | ~curl\_cffi.requests.impersonate.BrowserType | None \= None, ja3: str | None \= None, akamai: str | None \= None, extra\_fp: ~curl\_cffi.requests.impersonate.ExtraFingerprints | ~curl\_cffi.requests.impersonate.ExtraFpDict | None \= None, default\_headers: bool | None \= None, default\_encoding: str | ~typing.Callable\[\[bytes\], str\] \= 'utf-8', http\_version: ~curl\_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion | None \= None, interface: str | None \= None, cert: str | ~typing.Tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None, stream: bool \= False, max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0, multipart: ~curl\_cffi.curl.CurlMime | None \= None_) → [Response](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "curl_cffi.requests.models.Response")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.request "Link to this definition")

Send the request, see `requests.request` for details on parameters.

stream(_\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.stream)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.stream "Link to this definition")

Equivalent to `with request(..., stream=True) as r:`

ws\_connect(_url_, _\*args_, _on\_message: Callable\[\[[WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")\
, bytes\], None\] | None \= None_, _on\_error: Callable\[\[[WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")\
, CurlError\], None\] | None \= None_, _on\_open: Callable | None \= None_, _on\_close: Callable | None \= None_, _\*\*kwargs_) → [WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

Connects to a websocket url.

Parameters:

*   **url** – the ws url to connect.
    
*   **on\_message** – message callback, `def on_message(ws, str)`
    
*   **on\_error** – error callback, `def on_error(ws, error)`
    
*   **on\_open** – open callback, `def on_open(ws)`
    
*   **on\_close** – close callback, `def on_close(ws)`
    

Other parameters are the same as `.request`

Returns:

a ws instance to communicate with the server.

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.AsyncSession(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.aio.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "Link to this definition")

An async request session, cookies and connections will be reused.

\_\_init\_\_(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.aio.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the init method will be override by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **loop** – loop to use, if not provided, the running loop will be used.
    
*   **async\_curl** – \[AsyncCurl\](/api/curl\_cffi#curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl) object to use.
    
*   **max\_clients** – maxmium curl handle to use in the session, this will affect the concurrency ratio.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, format: {“http”: proxy\_url, “https”: proxy\_url}.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai strings.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager, and it’s recommended to use via `async with`. However, unlike aiohttp, it is not required to use `with`.

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

\# recommended.
async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

s \= AsyncSession()  \# it also works.

stream(_\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.stream)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.stream "Link to this definition")

Equivalent to `async with request(..., stream=True) as r:`

_async_ close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.close "Link to this definition")

Close the session.

_async_ ws\_connect(_url_, _\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

### Headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#headers "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Headers(_headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
 | Mapping\[str, str | None\] | Mapping\[bytes, bytes | None\] | Sequence\[Tuple\[str, str\]\] | Sequence\[Tuple\[bytes, bytes\]\] | Sequence\[str | bytes\] | None \= None_, _encoding: str | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "Link to this definition")

HTTP headers, as a case-insensitive multi-dict.

_property_ encoding_: str_[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.encoding "Link to this definition")

Header encoding is mandated as ascii, but we allow fallbacks to utf-8 or iso-8859-1.

raw()[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.raw "Link to this definition")

Returns a list of the raw header items, as byte pairs.

multi\_items() → List\[Tuple\[str, str\]\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.multi_items)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.multi_items "Link to this definition")

Return a list of (key, value) pairs of headers. Allow multiple occurrences of the same key without concatenating into a single comma separated value.

get(_key: str_, _default: Any \= None_) → Any[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get "Link to this definition")

Return a header value. If multiple occurrences of the header occur then concatenate them together with commas.

get\_list(_key: str_, _split\_commas: bool \= False_) → List\[str\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get_list "Link to this definition")

Return a list of all header values for a given key. If split\_commas=True is passed, then any comma separated header values are split into multiple return strings.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_key: str_) → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

Return a single header value. If there are multiple headers with the same key, then we concatenate them with commas. See: [https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2)

\_\_setitem\_\_(_key: str_, _value: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

Set the header key to value, removing any duplicate entries. Retains insertion order.

\_\_delitem\_\_(_key: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

Remove the header key.

### Cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#cookies "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Cookies(_cookies: [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "curl_cffi.requests.cookies.Cookies")
 | CookieJar | Dict\[str, str\] | List\[Tuple\[str, str\]\] | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "Link to this definition")

HTTP Cookies, as a mutable mapping.

set(_name: str_, _value: str_, _domain: str \= ''_, _path: str \= '/'_, _secure\=False_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.set)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.set "Link to this definition")

Set a cookie value by name. May optionally include domain and path.

get(_name: str_, _default: str | None \= None_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → str | None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.get "Link to this definition")

Get a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to retrieve.

delete(_name: str_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.delete)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.delete "Link to this definition")

Delete a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to delete.

clear(_domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.clear)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.clear "Link to this definition")

Delete all cookies. Optionally include a domain and path in order to only delete a subset of all the cookies.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_name: str_) → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_setitem\_\_(_name: str_, _value: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_delitem\_\_(_name: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

### Request, Response and WebSocket[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#request-response-and-websocket "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Request(_url: str_, _headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
_, _method: str_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "Link to this definition")

Representing a sent request.

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Response(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _request: [Request](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "curl_cffi.requests.models.Request")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "Link to this definition")

Contains information the server sends.

url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.url "Link to this definition")

url used in the request.

content[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.content "Link to this definition")

response body in bytes.

text[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.text "Link to this definition")

response body in str.

status\_code[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.status_code "Link to this definition")

http status code.

reason[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.reason "Link to this definition")

http response reason, such as OK, Not Found.

ok[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.ok "Link to this definition")

is status\_code in \[200, 400)?\
\
headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.headers "Link to this definition")\
\
response headers.\
\
cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.cookies "Link to this definition")\
\
response cookies.\
\
elapsed[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.elapsed "Link to this definition")\
\
how many seconds the request cost.\
\
encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
http body encoding.\
\
charset[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset "Link to this definition")\
\
alias for encoding.\
\
primary\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.primary_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
primary ip of the server.\
\
local\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.local_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
local ip used in this connection.\
\
charset\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding specified by the Content-Type header.\
\
default\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.default_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.\
\
redirect\_count[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_count "Link to this definition")\
\
how many redirects happened.\
\
redirect\_url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_url "Link to this definition")\
\
the final redirected url.\
\
http\_version[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.http_version "Link to this definition")\
\
http version used.\
\
history[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.history "Link to this definition")\
\
history redirections, only headers are available.\
\
raise\_for\_status()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.raise_for_status)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.raise_for_status "Link to this definition")\
\
Raise an error if status code is not in \[200, 400)\
\
iter\_lines(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_, _delimiter\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_lines)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_lines "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content line by line, separated by `\n`.\
\

iter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_content)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_content "Link to this definition")\

iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
json(_\*\*kw_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.json)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.json "Link to this definition")\

return a parsed json object of the content.\

[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.close "Link to this definition")\

Close the streaming connection, only valid in stream mode.\

\
iterate streaming content line by line, separated by `\n`.\

_async_ aiter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.aiter_content)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.aiter_content "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
_async_ atext() → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.atext)\

libcurl split frames into fragments, so we have to collect all the chunks for a frame.\
\
send(_payload: bytes_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")\

\

\

[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.arecv "Link to this definition")\
\

 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.asend)\

# API References — curl_cffi  documentation

API References[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#api-references "Link to this heading")

==============================================================================================================

curl low levl APIs[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl-low-levl-apis "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Curl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.Curl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_*` functions of libcurl.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _debug: bool \= False_, _handle\=None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, certs from `certifi` are used.
    
*   **debug** – whether to show curl debug messages.
    
*   **handle** – a curl handle instance from `curl_easy_init`.
    

debug() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.debug)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.debug "Link to this definition")

Set debug to True

setopt(_option: [CurlOpt](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlOpt "curl_cffi.const.CurlOpt")
_, _value: Any_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_setopt`.

Parameters:

*   **option** – option to set, using constants from CurlOpt enum
    
*   **value** – value to set, strings will be handled automatically
    

Returns:

0 if no error, see `CurlECode`.

getinfo(_option: [CurlInfo](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlInfo "curl_cffi.const.CurlInfo")
_) → bytes | int | float | list[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.getinfo)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.getinfo "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_getinfo`. Gets information in response after curl.perform.

Parameters:

**option** – option to get info of, using constants from `CurlInfo` enum

Returns:

value retrieved from last perform.

version() → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.version)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.version "Link to this definition")

Get the underlying libcurl version.

impersonate(_target: str_, _default\_headers: bool \= True_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.impersonate)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.impersonate "Link to this definition")

Set the browser type to impersonate.

Parameters:

*   **target** – browser to impersonate.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to add default headers, like User-Agent.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

perform(_clear\_headers: bool \= True_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.perform)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.perform "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_perform`, performs a curl request.

Parameters:

**clear\_headers** – clear header slist used in this perform

Raises:

**CurlError** – if the perform was not successful.

duphandle() → [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.duphandle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.duphandle "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for `curl_easy_duphandle`.

This is not a full copy of entire curl object in python. For example, headers handle is not copied, you have to set them again.

reset() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.reset)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.reset "Link to this definition")

Reset all curl options, wrapper for `curl_easy_reset`.

parse\_cookie\_headers(_headers: list\[bytes\]_) → SimpleCookie[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_cookie_headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_cookie_headers "Link to this definition")

Extract `cookies.SimpleCookie` from header lines.

Parameters:

**headers** – list of headers in bytes.

Returns:

A parsed cookies.SimpleCookie instance.

_static_ get\_reason\_phrase(_status\_line: bytes_) → bytes[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.get_reason_phrase)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.get_reason_phrase "Link to this definition")

Extract reason phrase, like `OK`, `Not Found` from response status line.

_static_ parse\_status\_line(_status\_line: bytes_) → tuple\[[CurlHttpVersion](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "curl_cffi.const.CurlHttpVersion")\
, int, bytes\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.parse_status_line)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.parse_status_line "Link to this definition")

Parse status line.

Returns:

http\_version, status\_code, and reason phrase

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup curl handle, wrapper for `curl_easy_cleanup`.

ws\_recv(_n: int \= 1024_) → tuple\[bytes, CurlWsFrame\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_recv)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_recv "Link to this definition")

Receive a frame from a websocket connection.

Parameters:

**n** – maximum data to receive.

Returns:

a tuple of frame content and curl frame meta struct.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_send(_payload: bytes_, _flags: [CurlWsFlag](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "curl_cffi.const.CurlWsFlag")
 \= CurlWsFlag.BINARY_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_send)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send "Link to this definition")

Send data to a websocket connection.

Parameters:

*   **payload** – content to send.
    
*   **flags** – websocket flag to set for the frame, default: binary.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

ws\_close(_code: int \= 1000_, _message: bytes \= b''_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#Curl.ws_close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_close "Link to this definition")

Close a websocket connection. Shorthand for [`ws_send()`](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send "curl_cffi.Curl.ws_send")
 with close code and message. Note that to completely close the connection, you must close the curl handle after this call with [`close()`](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl.close "curl_cffi.Curl.close")
.

Parameters:

*   **code** – close code.
    
*   **message** – close message.
    

Returns:

0 if no error.

Raises:

**CurlError** – if failed.

### AsyncCurl[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#asynccurl "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi handle to provide asyncio support. It uses the libcurl socket\_action APIs.

\_\_init\_\_(_cacert: str \= ''_, _loop\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **cacert** – CA cert path to use, by default, certs from `certifi` are used.
    
*   **loop** – EventLoop to use.
    

add\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.add_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.add_handle "Link to this definition")

Add a curl handle to be managed by curl\_multi. This is the equivalent of perform in the async world.

remove\_handle(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.remove_handle)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.remove_handle "Link to this definition")

Cancel a future for given curl handle.

set\_result(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_result)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_result "Link to this definition")

Mark a future as done for given curl handle.

set\_exception(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
_, _exception_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.set_exception)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.set_exception "Link to this definition")

Raise exception of a future for given curl handle.

setopt(_option_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.setopt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.setopt "Link to this definition")

Wrapper around curl\_multi\_setopt.

socket\_action(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_) → int[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.socket_action)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.socket_action "Link to this definition")

Call libcurl socket\_action function

process\_data(_sockfd: int_, _ev\_bitmask: int_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.process_data)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.process_data "Link to this definition")

Call curl\_multi\_info\_read to read data for given socket.

_async_ close()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/aio.html#AsyncCurl.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl.close "Link to this definition")

Close and cleanup running timers, readers, writers and handles.

### CurlMime[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curlmime "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlMime(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime "Link to this definition")

Wrapper for the `curl_mime_` API.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

**curl** – Curl instance to use.

addpart(_name: str_, _\*_, _content\_type: str | None \= None_, _filename: str | None \= None_, _local\_path: str | bytes | Path | None \= None_, _data: bytes | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.addpart)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.addpart "Link to this definition")

Add a mime part for a mutlipart html form.

Note: You can only use either local\_path or data, not both.

Parameters:

*   **name** – name of the field.
    
*   **content\_type** – content\_type for the field. for example: `image/png`.
    
*   **filename** – filename for the server.
    
*   **local\_path** – file to upload on local disk.
    
*   **data** – file content to upload.
    

_classmethod_ from\_list(_files: list\[dict\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.from_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.from_list "Link to this definition")

Create a multipart instance from a list of dict, for keys, see `addpart`

attach(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.attach)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.attach "Link to this definition")

Attach the mime instance to a curl instance.

close() → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/curl.html#CurlMime.close)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMime.close "Link to this definition")

Close the mime instance and underlying files. This method must be called after `perform` or `request`.

### Constants[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#constants "Link to this heading")

Enum values used by `setopt` and `getinfo` can be accessed from `CurlOpt` and `CurlInfo`.

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlOpt(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlOpt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlOpt "Link to this definition")

`CULROPT_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_easy\_setopt.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_easy_setopt.html)

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlInfo(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlInfo)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlInfo "Link to this definition")

`CURLINFO_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_easy\_getinfo.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_easy_getinfo.html)

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlMOpt(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlMOpt)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlMOpt "Link to this definition")

`CURLMOPT_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl\_multi\_setopt.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/curl_multi_setopt.html)

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlECode(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlECode)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlECode "Link to this definition")

`CURLECODE_` constancs extracted from libcurl, see: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-errors.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-errors.html)

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlHttpVersion(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlHttpVersion)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlHttpVersion "Link to this definition")

`CURL_HTTP_VERSION` constants from libcurl, see comments for details.

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlWsFlag(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlWsFlag)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlWsFlag "Link to this definition")

`CURL_WS_FLAG` constants from libcurl, see comments for details.

_class_ curl\_cffi.CurlSslVersion(_value_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/const.html#CurlSslVersion)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.CurlSslVersion "Link to this definition")

requests API[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#requests-api "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### request method[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#request-method "Link to this heading")

`requests.get`, `requests.post`, etc are just aliases of `.request(METHOD, ...)`

curl\_cffi.requests.request(_method: Literal\['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS', 'HEAD', 'TRACE', 'PATCH', 'QUERY'\]_, _url: str_, _thread: Literal\['eventlet', 'gevent'\] | None \= None_, _curl\_options: dict | None \= None_, _debug: bool | None \= None_, _\*\*kwargs: None_) → [Response](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "curl_cffi.requests.models.Response")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests.html#request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.request "Link to this definition")

Send an http request.

Parameters:

*   **method** – http method for the request: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE etc.
    
*   **url** – url for the requests.
    
*   **params** – query string for the requests.
    
*   **data** – form values(dict/list/tuple) or binary data to use in body, `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` will be added if a dict is given.
    
*   **json** – json values to use in body, Content-Type: application/json will be added automatically.
    
*   **headers** – headers to send.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.
    
*   **files** – not supported, use `multipart` instead.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.
    
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.
    
*   **content\_callback** – a callback function to receive response body. `def callback(chunk: bytes) -> None:`
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai strings.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers when impersonating.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.
    
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.
    
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.
    
*   **debug** – print extra curl debug info.
    
*   **interface** – which interface to use.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **stream** – streaming the response, default False.
    
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.
    
*   **multipart** – upload files using the multipart format, see examples for details.
    

Returns:

A `Response` object.

### Sessions[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#sessions "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Session(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: ThreadType | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session "Link to this definition")

A request session, cookies and connections will be reused. This object is thread-safe, but it’s recommended to use a separate session for each thread.

\_\_init\_\_(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _thread: ThreadType | None \= None_, _use\_thread\_local\_curl: bool \= True_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the `__init__` method will be overriden by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **curl** – curl object to use in the session. If not provided, a new one will be created. Also, a fresh curl object will always be created when accessed from another thread.
    
*   **thread** – thread engine to use for working with other thread implementations. choices: eventlet, gevent.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use proxy if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use as base for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **interface** – which interface use.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **response\_class** – A customized subtype of `Response` to use.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager.

from curl\_cffi.requests import Session

with Session() as s:
    r \= s.get("https://example.com")

'safari180', 'safari180\_ios', 'safari184', 'safari184\_ios', 'firefox133', 'firefox135', 'tor145', 'chrome', 'edge', 'safari', 'safari\_ios', 'chrome\_android', 'firefox', 'safari15\_3', 'safari15\_5', 'safari17\_0', 'safari17\_2\_ios', 'safari18\_0', 'safari18\_0\_ios', 'safari18\_4', 'safari18\_4\_ios'\] | None \= None, ja3: str | None \= None, akamai: str | None \= None, extra\_fp: tuple\[str, str\] | None \= None, stream: bool | None \= None, max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0, multipart: ~curl\_cffi.curl.CurlMime | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.request "Link to this definition")

Send the request, see `requests.request` for details on parameters.

stream(_method: Literal\['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'OPTIONS', 'HEAD', 'TRACE', 'PATCH', 'QUERY'\]_, _url: str_, _\*\*kwargs: None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.stream)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.stream "Link to this definition")

Equivalent to `with request(..., stream=True) as r:`

ws\_connect(_url_, _on\_message\=None_, _on\_error\=None_, _on\_open\=None_, _on\_close\=None_, _\*\*kwargs_) → [WebSocket](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "curl_cffi.requests.websockets.WebSocket")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#Session.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Session.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

Connects to a websocket url.

Note: This method is deprecated, use WebSocket instead.

Parameters:

*   **url** – the ws url to connect.
    
*   **on\_message** – message callback, `def on_message(ws, str)`
    
*   **on\_error** – error callback, `def on_error(ws, error)`
    
*   **on\_open** – open callback, `def on_open(ws)`
    
*   **on\_close** – close callback, `def on_close(ws)`
    

Other parameters are the same as `.request`

Returns:

a WebSocket instance to communicate with the server.

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.AsyncSession(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession "Link to this definition")

An async request session, cookies and connections will be reused.

\_\_init\_\_(_\*_, _loop\=None_, _async\_curl: [AsyncCurl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.AsyncCurl "curl_cffi.AsyncCurl")
 | None \= None_, _max\_clients: int \= 10_, _\*\*kwargs: Unpack\[BaseSessionParams\[R\]\]_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.__init__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.__init__ "Link to this definition")

Parameters set in the `__init__` method will be override by the same parameter in request method.

Parameters:

*   **loop** – loop to use, if not provided, the running loop will be used.
    
*   **async\_curl** – \[AsyncCurl\](/api/curl\_cffi#curl\_cffi.AsyncCurl) object to use.
    
*   **max\_clients** – maxmium curl handle to use in the session, this will affect the concurrency ratio.
    
*   **headers** – headers to use in the session.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to add in the session.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://proxy\_url](http://proxy_url/)
    ”. Cannot be used with the above parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **base\_url** – absolute url to use for relative urls.
    
*   **params** – query string for the session.
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **trust\_env** – use http\_proxy/https\_proxy and other environments, default True.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate in the session.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **response\_class** – A customized subtype of `Response` to use.
    

Notes

This class can be used as a context manager, and it’s recommended to use via `async with`. However, unlike aiohttp, it is not required to use `with`.

from curl\_cffi.requests import AsyncSession

\# recommended.
async with AsyncSession() as s:
    r \= await s.get("https://example.com")

s \= AsyncSession()  \# it also works.
