"curl_cffi.requests.websockets.AsyncWebSocket")
[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/session.html#AsyncSession.ws_connect)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.AsyncSession.ws_connect "Link to this definition")

Connects to a WebSocket.

Parameters:

*   **url** – url for the requests.
    
*   **autoclose** – whether to close the WebSocket after receiving a close frame.
    
*   **params** – query string for the requests.
    
*   **headers** – headers to send.
    
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.
    
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.
    
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.
    
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.
    
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.
    
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.
    
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.
    
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).
    
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.
    
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.
    
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.
    
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.
    
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.
    
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.
    
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.
    
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers.
    
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.
    
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.
    
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.
    
*   **interface** – which interface to use.
    
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.
    
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.
    

### Headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#headers "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Headers(_headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
 | Mapping\[str, str | None\] | Mapping\[bytes, bytes | None\] | Sequence\[tuple\[str, str\]\] | Sequence\[tuple\[bytes, bytes\]\] | Sequence\[str | bytes\] | None \= None_, _encoding: str | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "Link to this definition")

HTTP headers, as a case-insensitive multi-dict.

_property_ encoding_: str_[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.encoding "Link to this definition")

Header encoding is mandated as ascii, but we allow fallbacks to utf-8 or iso-8859-1.

raw()[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.raw "Link to this definition")

Returns a list of the raw header items, as byte pairs.

multi\_items() → list\[tuple\[str, str | None\]\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.multi_items)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.multi_items "Link to this definition")

Return a list of (key, value) pairs of headers. Allow multiple occurrences of the same key without concatenating into a single comma separated value.

get(_key: str_, _default: Any \= None_) → Any[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get "Link to this definition")

Return a header value. If multiple occurrences of the header occur then concatenate them together with commas.

get\_list(_key: str_, _split\_commas: bool \= False_) → list\[str | None\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.get_list)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.get_list "Link to this definition")

Return a list of all header values for a given key. If split\_commas=True is passed, then any comma separated header values are split into multiple return strings.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_key: str_) → str | None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

Return a single header value. If there are multiple headers with the same key, then we concatenate them with commas. See: [https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.2)

\_\_setitem\_\_(_key: str_, _value: str | None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

Set the header key to value, removing any duplicate entries. Retains insertion order.

\_\_delitem\_\_(_key: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/headers.html#Headers.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

Remove the header key.

### Cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#cookies "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Cookies(_cookies: [Cookies](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "curl_cffi.requests.cookies.Cookies")
 | CookieJar | dict\[str, str\] | list\[tuple\[str, str\]\] | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies "Link to this definition")

HTTP Cookies, as a mutable mapping.

set(_name: str_, _value: str_, _domain: str \= ''_, _path: str \= '/'_, _secure\=False_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.set)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.set "Link to this definition")

Set a cookie value by name. May optionally include domain and path.

get(_name: str_, _default: str | None \= None_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → str | None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.get)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.get "Link to this definition")

Get a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to retrieve.

delete(_name: str_, _domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.delete)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.delete "Link to this definition")

Delete a cookie by name. May optionally include domain and path in order to specify exactly which cookie to delete.

clear(_domain: str | None \= None_, _path: str | None \= None_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.clear)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.clear "Link to this definition")

Delete all cookies. Optionally include a domain and path in order to only delete a subset of all the cookies.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from mapping/iterable E and F.[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.update)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.update "Link to this definition")

If E present and has a .keys() method, does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E present and lacks .keys() method, does: for (k, v) in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k, v in F.items(): D\[k\] = v

\_\_getitem\_\_(_name: str_) → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__getitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__getitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_setitem\_\_(_name: str_, _value: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__setitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__setitem__ "Link to this definition")

\_\_delitem\_\_(_name: str_) → None[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/cookies.html#Cookies.__delitem__)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Cookies.__delitem__ "Link to this definition")

### Request, Response and WebSocket[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#request-response-and-websocket "Link to this heading")

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Request(_url: str_, _headers: [Headers](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Headers "curl_cffi.requests.headers.Headers")
_, _method: str_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Request)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "Link to this definition")

Representing a sent request.

_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.Response(_curl: [Curl](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.Curl "curl_cffi.curl.Curl")
 | None \= None_, _request: [Request](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Request "curl_cffi.requests.models.Request")
 | None \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response)
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response "Link to this definition")

Contains information the server sends.

url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.url "Link to this definition")

url used in the request.

content[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.content "Link to this definition")

response body in bytes.

text[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.text "Link to this definition")

response body in str.

status\_code[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.status_code "Link to this definition")

http status code.

reason[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.reason "Link to this definition")

http response reason, such as OK, Not Found.

ok[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.ok "Link to this definition")

is status\_code in \[200, 400)?\
\
headers[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.headers "Link to this definition")\
\
response headers.\
\
cookies[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.cookies "Link to this definition")\
\
response cookies.\
\
elapsed[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.elapsed "Link to this definition")\
\
how many seconds the request cost.\
\
encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
http body encoding.\
\
charset[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset "Link to this definition")\
\
alias for encoding.\
\
primary\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.primary_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
primary ip of the server.\
\
primary\_port[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.primary_port "Link to this definition")\
\
primary port of the server.\
\
local\_ip[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.local_ip "Link to this definition")\
\
local ip used in this connection.\
\
local\_port[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.local_port "Link to this definition")\
\
local port used in this connection.\
\
charset\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.charset_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding specified by the Content-Type header.\
\
default\_encoding[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.default_encoding "Link to this definition")\
\
encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.\
\
redirect\_count[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_count "Link to this definition")\
\
how many redirects happened.\
\
redirect\_url[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.redirect_url "Link to this definition")\
\
the final redirected url.\
\
http\_version[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.http_version "Link to this definition")\
\
http version used.\
\
history[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.history "Link to this definition")\
\
history redirections, only headers are available.\
\
raise\_for\_status()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.raise_for_status)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.raise_for_status "Link to this definition")\
\
Raise an error if status code is not in \[200, 400)\
\
iter\_lines(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_, _delimiter\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_lines)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_lines "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content line by line, separated by `\n`.\
\

 which is under the License: Apache 2.0\
\
iter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.iter_content)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.iter_content "Link to this definition")\
\
iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
json(_\*\*kw_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.json)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.json "Link to this definition")\

return a parsed json object of the content.\
\
close()[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.close)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.Response.close "Link to this definition")\
\
Close the streaming connection, only valid in stream mode.\
\
_async_ aiter\_lines(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_, _delimiter\=None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.aiter_lines)\

_async_ aiter\_content(_chunk\_size\=None_, _decode\_unicode\=False_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.aiter_content)\

iterate streaming content chunk by chunk in bytes.\
\
_async_ atext() → str[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/models.html#Response.atext)\

Return a decoded string.\

\
_class_ curl\_cffi.requests.WebSocket(_curl: Union\[Curl_, _Any\] \= <object object>_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _skip\_utf8\_validation: bool \= False_, _debug: bool \= False_, _on\_open: Optional\[ON\_OPEN\_T\] \= None_, _on\_close: Optional\[ON\_CLOSE\_T\] \= None_, _on\_data: Optional\[ON\_DATA\_T\] \= None_, _on\_message: Optional\[ON\_MESSAGE\_T\] \= None_, _on\_error: Optional\[ON\_ERROR\_T\] \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket "Link to this definition")\
\
A WebSocket implementation using libcurl.\
\
\_\_init\_\_(_curl: Union\[Curl_, _Any\] \= <object object>_, _\*_, _autoclose: bool \= True_, _skip\_utf8\_validation: bool \= False_, _debug: bool \= False_, _on\_open: Optional\[ON\_OPEN\_T\] \= None_, _on\_close: Optional\[ON\_CLOSE\_T\] \= None_, _on\_data: Optional\[ON\_DATA\_T\] \= None_, _on\_message: Optional\[ON\_MESSAGE\_T\] \= None_, _on\_error: Optional\[ON\_ERROR\_T\] \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.__init__)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.__init__ "Link to this definition")\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **autoclose** – whether to close the WebSocket after receiving a close frame.\
    \
*   **skip\_utf8\_validation** – whether to skip UTF-8 validation for text frames in run\_forever().\
    \
*   **debug** – print extra curl debug info.\
    \
*   **on\_open** – open callback, `def on_open(ws)`\
    \
*   **on\_close** – close callback, `def on_close(ws, code, reason)`\
    \
*   **on\_data** – raw data receive callback, `def on_data(ws, data, frame)`\
    \
*   **on\_message** – message receive callback, `def on_message(ws, message)`\
    \
*   **on\_error** – error callback, `def on_error(ws, exception)`\
    \
\
connect(_url: str, params: Optional\[Union\[dict, list, tuple\]\] \= None, headers: Optional\[HeaderTypes\] \= None, cookies: Optional\[CookieTypes\] \= None, auth: Optional\[tuple\[str, str\]\] \= None, timeout: Optional\[Union\[float, tuple\[float, float\], object\]\] \= <object object>, allow\_redirects: bool \= True, max\_redirects: int \= 30, proxies: Optional\[ProxySpec\] \= None, proxy: Optional\[str\] \= None, proxy\_auth: Optional\[tuple\[str, str\]\] \= None, verify: Optional\[bool\] \= None, referer: Optional\[str\] \= None, accept\_encoding: Optional\[str\] \= 'gzip, deflate, br', impersonate: Optional\[BrowserTypeLiteral\] \= None, ja3: Optional\[str\] \= None, akamai: Optional\[str\] \= None, extra\_fp: Optional\[Union\[ExtraFingerprints, ExtraFpDict\]\] \= None, default\_headers: bool \= True, quote: Union\[str, Literal\[False\]\] \= '', http\_version: Optional\[CurlHttpVersion\] \= None, interface: Optional\[str\] \= None, cert: Optional\[Union\[str, tuple\[str, str\]\]\] \= None, max\_recv\_speed: int \= 0, curl\_options: Optional\[dict\[CurlOpt, str\]\] \= None_)[\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.connect)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.connect "Link to this definition")\
\
Connect to the WebSocket.\
\
libcurl automatically handles pings and pongs. ref: [https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html](https://curl.se/libcurl/c/libcurl-ws.html)\
\
Parameters:\
\
*   **url** – url for the requests.\
    \
*   **params** – query string for the requests.\
    \
*   **headers** – headers to send.\
    \
*   **cookies** – cookies to use.\
    \
*   **auth** – HTTP basic auth, a tuple of (username, password), only basic auth is supported.\
    \
*   **timeout** – how many seconds to wait before giving up.\
    \
*   **allow\_redirects** – whether to allow redirection.\
    \
*   **max\_redirects** – max redirect counts, default 30, use -1 for unlimited.\
    \
*   **proxies** – dict of proxies to use, prefer to use `proxy` if they are the same. format: `{"http": proxy_url, "https": proxy_url}`.\
    \
*   **proxy** – proxy to use, format: “[http://user@pass:proxy\_url](http://user@pass:proxy_url)\
    ”. Can’t be used with proxies parameter.\
    \
*   **proxy\_auth** – HTTP basic auth for proxy, a tuple of (username, password).\
    \
*   **verify** – whether to verify https certs.\
    \
*   **referer** – shortcut for setting referer header.\
    \
*   **accept\_encoding** – shortcut for setting accept-encoding header.\
    \
*   **impersonate** – which browser version to impersonate.\
    \
*   **ja3** – ja3 string to impersonate.\
    \
*   **akamai** – akamai string to impersonate.\
    \
*   **extra\_fp** – extra fingerprints options, in complement to ja3 and akamai str.\
    \
*   **default\_headers** – whether to set default browser headers.\
    \
*   **default\_encoding** – encoding for decoding response content if charset is not found in headers. Defaults to “utf-8”. Can be set to a callable for automatic detection.\
    \
*   **quote** – Set characters to be quoted, i.e. percent-encoded. Default safe string is `!#$%&'()*+,/:;=?@[]~`. If set to a sting, the character will be removed from the safe string, thus quoted. If set to False, the url will be kept as is, without any automatic percent-encoding, you must encode the URL yourself.\
    \
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.\
    \
*   **http\_version** – limiting http version, defaults to http2.\
    \
*   **interface** – which interface to use.\
    \
*   **cert** – a tuple of (cert, key) filenames for client cert.\
    \
*   **max\_recv\_speed** – maximum receive speed, bytes per second.\
    \
*   **curl\_options** – extra curl options to use.\
    \
\
recv\_fragment() → tuple\[bytes, CurlWsFrame\][\[source\]](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/_modules/curl_cffi/requests/websockets.html#WebSocket.recv_fragment)\
[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv_fragment "Link to this definition")\

libcurl splits frames into fragments, so we have to collect all the chunks for a frame.\

[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.11.0/api.html#curl_cffi.requests.WebSocket.recv_json "Link to this definition")\

 for details on parameters.\

# Compatibility with requests — curl_cffi  documentation

Compatibility with requests[¶](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/vs-requests.html#compatibility-with-requests "Link to this heading")

===============================================================================================================================================

Although we try our best to mimic the requests API, some functionality is not easy to implement and left out. Here are a list of known incompatibilities:

*   files API are slightly different, but more error-proof.
    
*   retries are not supported yet, tracked in \[#24\]([https://github.com/yifeikong/curl\_cffi/issues/24](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/issues/24)
    )
    
*   redirect history are not supported, tracked in \[#82\]([https://github.com/yifeikong/curl\_cffi/issues/82](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/issues/82)
    )
    
*   empty-domains cookies may lost during redirects, tracked in \[#55\]([https://github.com/yifeikong/curl\_cffi/issues/55](https://github.com/yifeikong/curl_cffi/issues/55)
    )
    

[curl\_cffi](https://curl-cffi.readthedocs.io/en/v0.7.0/index.html)
